keyPoints: Big O notation is used to describe the time complexity of algorithms like Bubble Sort and Selection Sort.
Bubble Sort works by repeatedly swapping adjacent elements if they are in the wrong order.
Selection Sort works by repeatedly finding the minimum element from the unsorted part of the list and swapping it with the first unsorted element.
Insertion Sort works by iterating through the list one element at a time, inserting each element into its proper position in the sorted part of the list.
